# Rojas Report

> Standing intelligence on who pays whom in American healthcare. A reference console, not a newsfeed, for the lobbyist, the physician, the lawmaker, and the voter.

The Rojas Report is the central hub for 23 desks of standing healthcare-policy intelligence. It maps the relationships between trade associations, payers, manufacturers, hospitals, physicians, lobbyists, donors, lawmakers, and the laws that bind them.

## Core reference

- [All 23 desks](https://rojasreport.com/desks/): The full registry of healthcare-policy desks
- [Method](https://rojasreport.com/method/): How sources are gathered, verified, and cited
- [About](https://rojasreport.com/about/): Editorial mission and provenance

## Live desks

- [Ways & Means](https://waysandmeans.rojasreport.com): Tax writing and entitlement financing
- [POH](https://poh.rojasreport.com): Physician-owned hospitals (189 tracked, 32 states)
- [Academic](https://academic.rojasreport.com): AMCs, NIH grants, residency, teaching payments
- [FAH](https://fah.rojasreport.com): Federation of American Hospitals
- [AHA](https://aha.rojasreport.com): American Hospital Association
- [HAC](https://hac.rojasreport.com): Healthcare Advisory Committees
- [CON Laws](https://con-laws.rojasreport.com): State Certificate of Need regimes

## For readers

- [The Lobbyist](https://rojasreport.com/readers/lobbyist/): Who does AHIP pay, and who pays AHIP?
- [The Physician](https://rojasreport.com/readers/physician/): Can I build a micro-hospital next to this POH?
- [The Lawmaker](https://rojasreport.com/readers/lawmaker/): Who could be an ally on 340B reform?
- [The Voter](https://rojasreport.com/readers/voter/): What is Abbott Labs spending in my district?
